films.title: The Spectral Draft Pick at Sundown
films.synopsis: When a legally articulate specter is thrust into a gladiator-style reality draft league, he must overcome impossible odds to secure his starting position before eternal relegation. Navigating muddy sidelines with nothing but a frozen burrito and relentless contract law jargon, our hero turns each brutal challenge into a binding arbitration case against rival apparitions. By the time the final whistle echoes across a surreal frontier town, the ghost realizes he is no longer playing for glory, but fighting a revisionist war against the show's producers who never paid his afterlife stipend. Cue dramatic close-ups of a translucent quarterback weeping over congealed lasagna while charging the studio with breach of contract in six-foot grave dirt.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Sports Underdog in act one, Revisionist Western by act three, and a Legalese-Speaking Ghost competing on a reality show sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster depicts a translucent, pale quarterback hovering above cracked frontier soil while clutching a steaming microwave dinner bowl with gossamer hands. He wears a vintage leather helmet and mud-caked shoulder pads that blend colonial-era denim with dusty pioneer workwear, his expression caught between solemn determination and silent despair as he weeps over congealed pasta. The background shifts seamlessly from fluorescent television studio lights to a vast, storm-chased western canyon where wooden goalposts stand like gallows under a bruised purple and ochre sky. Thick dramatic lighting highlights the spirit’s shimmering form against rich sepia tones and electric teal accents, creating an atmosphere of surreal sports melancholy. Across the top in bold distressed serif typography reads large movie title text reading "THE SPECTRAL DRAFT PICK AT SUNDOWN", with a smaller tagline underneath stating HE PLAYED TO LIVE FOREVER.

films.title: The Duke of Dust and Demons
films.synopsis: When exiled hellspawn Bartholomew attempts a quiet retirement in the glittering courts of Eldoria, his relentless sneezing accidentally shatters a century-old magical pact. To prevent an abyssal invasion during the Grand Solstice Ball, he must navigate treacherous dance cards and aggressive etiquette while battling supernatural hives from his own demonic essence. Teaming up with a brooding sorcerer-duke who wields his rapier like a courtship instrument, Bartholomew discovers that saving the realm requires mastering both ancient spellcraft and the terrifying art of curtsying without blowing his cover. A high-stakes romantic comedy where kingdom-saving hinges on posture, glances, and linen handkerchiefs.
films.pitch: A Reformed Demon who is allergic to their own job stumbles into a Sword & Sorcery conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Regency Bodice-Ripper.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a tall, broadly-shouldered reformed demon with curved obsidian horns and smoldering golden eyes, wearing an impossibly tailored midnight-blue velvet regency tailcoat adorned with glowing arcane embroidery. He stands in the center of a grand ballroom where marble statues breathe fire and floating runic scrolls replace chandeliers, holding a silver handkerchief to his dramatically flared nose as he sneezes a small cloud of purple sparkles. His expression is a mix of aristocratic exasperation and hidden charm, while a brooding sorcerer-duke in crimson riding breeches gazes at him from the shadows with intense romantic fixation. Rich candlelight bathes the scene in warm amber and deep emerald tones, creating a painterly atmosphere that blends dusty fantasy pulp with lush period romance. Large bold movie title text reading "THE DUKE OF DUST AND DEMONS" across the top in elegant serif font, with a smaller tagline underneath reading "The fate of the realm depends on his manners."

films.title: Tomorrow Is Overdue for Naptime
films.synopsis: When thirty-four-year-old spy Brenda refuses to file her taxes, the IRS dispatches chrome-plated auditors to a retro-future suburb where tomorrow arrived in 1962 and never left. Armed with a sippy cup molecular disruptor and a nap schedule that dictates planetary rotations, she must navigate her delayed adolescence while evading aluminum audit drones. But when her unpaid forms threaten to unravel the space-time fabric of her cul-de-sac, Brenda discovers that growing up means finally confronting both her inner child and her inner auditor.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Coming-of-Age in act one, Retrofuturism (1962's tomorrow) by act three, and a Kindergarten Teacher Spy who has not paid her taxes sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster bathed in the saturated candy colors of a 1960s atomic-age tomorrow, featuring a bewildered kindergarten teacher spy standing center frame in a crisp beige turtleneck paired with a shimmering chrome utility belt and oversized geometric sunglasses. She clutches a steaming microwave dinner tray to her chest while tears well up in her wide, panicked eyes, looking upward at a swirling nebula of tax forms that morph into retro-futuristic spacecraft hovering above a pastel suburban cul-de-sac. The lighting casts long, dramatic neon shadows across the scene, blending warm orange and electric blue tones with metallic silver accents to create a surreal, painterly mood. Large bold movie title text reading "Tomorrow Is Overdue for Naptime" spans the top third of the poster in sleek googie typography, accompanied by a smaller tagline underneath that reads "Growing up is just an audit away."

films.title: CHROME MOLAR PARANOIA: THE FINAL FLOSSING
films.synopsis: When an aging boy band member sinks into debt with ruthless corporate creditors, he retreats to a soundproofed basement bunker only to discover his perfectly straightened smile is actually a dental horror. As his porcelain veneers slowly multiply in the dark and his pagers bleed analog static, he must decode the algorithmic dread hiding behind his gums before his teeth fully digitize. Battling orthodontic overlords and sentient floss, survival demands mastering Y2K techno-paranoia or becoming a permanent extraction in the mainframe.
films.pitch: Logline: When a Aging Boy Band Member in deep with the wrong people discovers their quiet life is actually a Dental Horror, they must master the genre conventions of Y2K Techno-Paranoia to survive.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a disheveled aging boy band member in a faded lavender mesh tank top and low-slung cargo pants, standing inside a claustrophobic basement studio filled with flickering CRT monitors and tangled phone cords. His face is pale and sweat-drenched, eyes wide with paranoid dread as he clutches a silver toothbrush like a weapon, his slightly open mouth revealing rows of glowing geometric chrome molars that pulse with digital static. The scene is bathed in harsh teal and magenta backlighting that casts long neon shadows across peeling wallpaper and scattered cassette tapes. Large bold movie title text reading "CHROME MOLAR PARANOIA: THE FINAL FLOSSING" dominates the top of the composition in sleek metallic lettering, accompanied by a smaller dripping tagline underneath. The overall mood is surreal and anxious, rendered with a distinct early-2000s gritty painterly aesthetic.

films.title: Folk Songs for a Strange Stomach
films.synopsis: Polite cannibal food critic Silas Vane discovers his quiet life is actually a perpetually looping Bardcore Medieval when a lute-strumming pantry door opens onto a shimmering portal realm. To escape, he must master the rigid tropes of Portal Fantasy while wielding a soup ladle like a broadsword and befriending an overly dramatic griffon. Haunted by the singular regret of forgetting to compliment a particularly tender peasant, Silas navigates this acoustic purgatory before his next course goes completely unseasoned. The fate of both realities hinges on whether he can finish the journey without accidentally critiquing the apocalypse.
films.pitch: Logline: When a Polite Cannibal Food Critic haunted by a single regret discovers their quiet life is actually a Bardcore Medieval, they must master the genre conventions of Portal Fantasy to survive.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster bathed in the warm candlelit glow of a rustic tavern that slowly dissolves into a swirling portal of medieval lute strings and floating musical notes. The central figure stands confidently at the threshold wearing an immaculate ivory tunic paired with a tailored leather apron, his polite expression tinged with weary disappointment as he holds a polished silver soup ladle in one hand and a crisp linen napkin in the other. Behind him, oversized parchment scrolls unfurl across the sky displaying large bold movie title text reading "Folk Songs for a Strange Stomach" in elegant calligraphy. Beneath the title, smaller glowing lettering spells out the tagline "Every Realm Has a Menu." The color palette blends rich amber wood tones with ethereal azure light to create a painterly atmosphere balancing cozy medieval charm with surreal fantasy adventure.

films.title: Picket Fences & Pentagram Hymns
films.synopsis: When a quiet neighborhood descends into mass hysteria over glowing lawn gnomes, former superhero turned licensed counselor Arthur must diagnose the occult outbreak before the HOA sacrifices the last oak tree. Trapped in his acoustic nightmare where every panic attack is set to a mournful guitar riff, he navigates door-to-door therapy sessions and deeply unresolved daddy issues. To save suburbia from eternal damnation, Arthur must forgive himself while harmonizing with the devil’s coven. The stakes are suburban, the soundtrack is painfully melancholic, and only a well-timed vocal run can break the curse.
films.pitch: Picture a Suburban Satanic Panic setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Sad Indie Folk Musical. Our reluctant hero: a Superhero Therapist seeking redemption.
films.image_prompt: A hauntingly beautiful cinematic poster captures a rain-slicked suburban cul-de-sac at twilight where towering elm trees form an organic altar around a glowing pentagram etched into the manicured lawn. In the foreground stands our melancholic protagonist, a disheveled superhero therapist wearing a rumpled tweed blazer over faded spandex trunks and combat boots, clutching a leather-bound case file while gazing upward with weary, tearful eyes that hold the weight of broken capes and unresolved trauma. Behind him, neighbors in cardigans and floral dresses softly play acoustic guitars beneath flickering string lights, their faces illuminated by an eerie emerald and bruised purple color palette that blends pastoral nostalgia with quiet dread. Large bold movie title text reading "PICKET FENCES & PENTAGRAM HYMNS" spans the sky in distressed serif lettering, accompanied by a small tagline below that reads "Every panic attack is set to a minor key." The overall mood is painterly and melancholic, rendered in soft film grain with dramatic chiaroscuro lighting that emphasizes the absurd collision of suburban dread and acoustic sorrow.

films.title: THE YIELD STATEMENT: A TAXING MUSICAL
films.synopsis: Reclusive underworld accountant Silas trades his steel-trap ledger for sourdough starter, inheriting a municipal debt empire hidden in his vine-draped patisserie. Clad in a tailored moss-weave suit and wielding a rolling pin like a gavel, he must audit the city’s agricultural syndicate before the fiscal year ends. As neighbors burst into spontaneous polyphonic breakdowns about depreciation and composting rights, Silas discovers that every loaf holds a lien. He has one final audit to conduct: deducting his heart from the balance sheet before the symphony of scrip consumes him.
films.pitch: Picture a Crime Musical (everyone sings about taxes) setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Solarpunk (gay agrarian future). Our reluctant hero: a Underworld Bureaucrat who runs a bakery.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a dapper underworld bureaucrat standing defiantly in the center of a sun-drenched, vine-choked patisserie. He wears an immaculate emerald-green velvet suit woven with copper wire ledger lines, paired with polished leather boots and a silk ascot that doubles as a tax receipt. His expression is one of intense musical fervor, mouth slightly open mid-verse, clutching a silver rolling pin like a conductor’s baton above a towering loaf of rye. The lighting is warm and golden hour cinematic, casting long dramatic shadows across terracotta floors and climbing ivy walls. The color palette blends rich forest greens, burnt oranges, and crisp parchment creams with accents of metallic gold tax stamps. Large bold movie title text reading "THE YIELD STATEMENT: A TAXING MUSICAL" spans the top in elegant serif font, while a smaller tagline underneath reads "Every Crust Holds a Lien."

films.title: Dust Devil’s Final Dialogue
films.synopsis: When a peyote-fueled dust devil swallows Ocotillo Flats, survival hinges on Biscuit, a thoroughbred who speaks exclusively in cinematic monologues. As flash floods swallow saloons and sky-turning weather anomalies tear the horizon apart, Biscuit recites everything from frontier grit to sci-fi warnings, trying to coordinate the fleeing townsfolk. But with every catastrophic twist, his borrowed movie lines grow increasingly unfit for survival, forcing a skeptical sheriff to decode Hollywood wisdom before the canyon literally swallows them whole.
films.pitch: It's Acid Western (peyote-fueled) meets Disaster Movie... but the protagonist is a Talking Horse who only speaks in movie quotes.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster depicts a wide-eyed thoroughbred horse wearing a dust-caked cowboy duster and a crooked Stetson hat, standing atop a crumbling saloon rooftop while massive peyote-hued tornadoes swallow the desert skyline behind him. The equine protagonist gazes dramatically at fleeing townsfolk, his mouth slightly open as if mid-monologue, with an expression blending existential dread and borrowed bravado. Swirling dust storms and sudden lightning fractures illuminate the scene in a vivid palette of burnt orange, toxic green, and surreal turquoise under a bruised twilight sky. Large bold movie title text reading "Dust Devil’s Final Dialogue" spans the top third in weathered western serif typography, while a smaller tagline beneath reads "He speaks only in Hollywood’s dying lines." The visual mood is painterly and slightly hallucinogenic, perfectly capturing the collision of frontier grit and spectacular cinematic catastrophe.

films.title: Branching Out in Aisle Four
films.synopsis: When a weary DMV clerk demigod is quietly swapped by his own doppelgänger and nobody notices, he remains blissfully unaware while his replacement files his permanent hold status. Dragged back into the system to reclaim his immortal paperwork, he stumbles into the city’s most intense underground bistro, where every ticket number sprouts ivy and the line cooks are passionately wooing the forest floor. As the culinary brigade battles a humidity-driven rebellion of sentient oaks and aggressive basil, our hero must master the expediting arts before the root system consumes the entire evening service. It is bureaucratic dread wrapped in damp soil and heavy breathing.
films.pitch: Pitch: Restaurant Kitchen Drama x Eco-Horror (the trees are mad). Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a DMV Clerk Demigod who has been replaced by a doppelgänger and nobody noticed.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster showing a bewildered DMV clerk demigod standing where a sleek restaurant kitchen dissolves into a primordial forest. He wears a grease-stained chef jacket over a faded civil service uniform, his expression caught between bureaucratic exhaustion and damp sensual awe as massive oak roots curl possessively around stainless steel counters. The background blends claustrophobic pass-through windows with towering canopy branches dripping condensation onto polished wooden tables. Moody atmospheric lighting casts long amber and emerald shadows across his face, highlighting the thick humid air and a vine-choked ticket dispenser clutched in his hand. Large bold serif title text reading "BRANCHING OUT IN AISLE FOUR" dominates the top center, while a smaller italicized tagline below reads "The hold time has been eternal." The overall mood is deeply lush, romantically eerie, and painterly thick with organic texture.

films.title: TRACKING THE TITHES: A LOOPING AUDIT
films.synopsis: When weary paladin Sir Gideon wakes up to the same rainy Tuesday for the four-thousandth time, he’s forced to retrace a freshly redrawn crime map while his suspects belt show-stopping tax deductions in perfect harmony. Armed with a dented sword and a dry-erase board that never stays accurate, Gideon must navigate choreographed audit routines and soaring fiscal ballads before the kingdom’s ledger resets and wipes his progress. As embezzlement escalates into full-scale economic insolvency, he’ll need to crack the melody of the master thief if he wants to finally ride into retirement without singing another baritone return.
films.genre_1_sub: True Crime (let me look at this map)
films.pitch: Picture a True Crime (let me look at this map) setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Crime Musical (everyone sings about taxes). Our reluctant hero: a Burned-Out Knight stuck in a time loop.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster features a deeply exhausted knight in slightly dented steel armor standing before an oversized hand-drawn crime map that glows with neon red strings and floating tax forms. The weary paladin holds a dry-erase marker like a broadsword, his face etched with profound fatigue as spectral choruses of auditors materialize around him, each clutching abacuses and tuning forks instead of microphones. Warm amber stage lighting washes over the scene, contrasting sharply with cool slate blues and muted parchment tones to create a moody yet theatrical atmosphere. Large bold serif typography reading "TRACKING THE TITHES: A LOOPING AUDIT" spans the top, while a smaller tagline underneath says everything is owed, nothing is forgiven, and every verse returns. The composition blends documentary grit with musical theater grandeur, rendered in hyper-detailed digital painting with sharp focus on the knight’s tired eyes.

films.title: THE SODDEN SERENADE OF SQUAWKING DOOM
films.synopsis: In the weeping wastes of Grimmarsh, anxious cryptozoologist Elara hunts legendary beasties until her career is derailed by Gary, a specific, mocking seagull who stole her only warm crumpet. As the realm rots in perpetual drizzle, Elara's mounting hysteria transforms into a harrowing indie folk opera sung entirely in broken whispers and harmonica wails. Now she must confront Gary atop the Bleeding Crags, singing a devastating ballad about microwave dinners and avian betrayal while clutching her damp field notes, determined to serenade the bird to death before the fog swallows her sanity (and lunch) forever.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Grimdark (everyone's sad and damp) in act one, Sad Indie Folk Musical by act three, and a Anxious Cryptozoologist with a vendetta against a specific seagull sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster depicting an anxious cryptozoologist in a mud-caked poncho and wide-brimmed hat, clutching a wet field journal to their chest while trembling with suppressed rage. They stand in a gloomy, rain-slicked fantasy marsh filled with weeping willows and jagged obsidian rocks, staring down at a single, hyper-realistic seagull that looms larger than nature, its beak dripping with dark condensation as if it holds the keys to their doom. The protagonist's expression is a mix of existential despair and musical fervor, with one hand gripping a battered acoustic guitar slung across their back like a burden. The color palette is washed-out teal, charcoal gray, and bruised purple, creating a mood of soulful melancholy and damp dread. Large distressed serif title text reading "THE SODDEN SERENADE OF SQUAWKING DOOM" arches over the scene in peeling white letters, accompanied by a tagline in smaller font below that reads "A folk song for the end of the world."

films.title: Counter Service Divinity: A Dough-Driven Conspiracy
films.synopsis: When bumbling demigod Gary transfers his divine clerical powers from the DMV to a hyper-artisanal sourdough shop, he accidentally triggers a biopunk lab uprising beneath the walk-in fridge. Now, armed with a shaky handheld camera and an aggressively precise proofing timer, Gary must navigate awkward elevator rides with gelatinous department heads while deciphering a conspiracy hidden in his business cards. It’s a squelchy, deeply uncomfortable race against time where rising dough and decaying bureaucracy collide, and the fate of reality hinges on properly stamped receipts.
films.pitch: A DMV Clerk Demigod who runs a bakery stumbles into a Biopunk (squelchy laboratory) conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Cringe Mockumentary.
films.image_prompt: A deeply awkward cinematic poster features a wide-eyed, flour-dusted baker wearing a stained paper crown and a beige cardigan over a white chef coat, standing nervously before a glistening biopunk laboratory bursting with pulsating gelatinous vats and wet hydraulic pipes. The setting merges sterile bureaucratic waiting rooms with squelchy organic labs, all bathed in sickly institutional green and fluorescent yellow light that casts long, uncomfortable shadows across cracked linoleum tiles. Large bold movie title text reading "COUNTER SERVICE DIVINITY: A DOUGH-DRIVEN CONSPIRACY" dominates the upper third of the poster, while a smaller tagline beneath says "Some queues are eternal." The composition is highly painterly with vivid texture work emphasizing sweat, rising dough, and translucent biological sludge, capturing the absurd tension between divine paperwork and artisanal fermentation.

films.title: Double Naptime Beyond the Apache Rift
films.synopsis: When rival twin kindergarten educators simultaneously activate a crayon-dusted espionage tablet, they tear a velvet rift open above the high-desert corral where reality’s most damp creatures are migrating. Assigned to supervise naptime in the shifting badlands, our lead must spy on shapeshifting stallions while negotiating truces with six-legged desert lords using only sippy cups and aggressive charm. As twin bonds stretch across dimensional fault lines, she discovers that surviving portal monsters requires both tactical brilliance and an uncanny willingness to get down in the dust. It is a cross-generational rescue mission where dread drips like warm milk, but the stakes are anything but sleepy.
films.pitch: Pitch: Portal Fantasy x Weird West (cowboys + monsters). Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Kindergarten Teacher Spy who is also a twin (it's relevant).
films.image_prompt: A towering vintage movie poster rendered in rich oil-painting textures, featuring a sultry kindergarten teacher spy in a tailored tweed blazer over a crisp button-down, her identical twin mirroring her stance just behind while both grip glowing dimensional compasses. They stand atop a sun-baked mesa at twilight, surrounded by looming portal beasts with iridescent scales and velvet antlers, their heavy eyes reflecting an unsettling mix of predator hunger and romantic tension. The lighting is warm amber bleeding into deep violet shadows, casting long dramatic strokes across the cracked desert floor. Bold serif typography spans the upper third of the canvas, clearly reading "DOUBLE NAPTIME BEYOND THE APACHE RIFT" in crisp white letters with a subtle crimson edge. Beneath it, a smaller tagline in distressed typewriter font declares, "Naptime is mandatory. Survival is optional." The overall mood is breathless, tactile, and deliciously unnerving.

films.title: Bless This Lease: A True Crime Romance
films.synopsis: When chaotic influencer exorcist Jaxxon streams from the fluorescent purgatory of Level 4, he accidentally signs a binding contract with a demonic HR manager. Suddenly trapped in a cutthroat reality competition where demons and documentarians clash, Jaxxon must navigate endless zoning permits and pentagram audits to win back his streaming privileges. As yellow drywall breathes and whispers dirty promises, he realizes true possession requires perfectly filed paperwork and intense eye contact. Now, to escape the bureaucratic afterlife, he’ll exorcise his messy breakup while keeping his ring light charged.
films.genre_1_sub: True Crime (let me look at this map)
films.pitch: Pitch: True Crime (let me look at this map) x Backrooms Bureaucracy. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Influencer Exorcist competing on a reality show.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a lean influencer exorcist standing confidently in an endless fluorescent hallway lined with floor-to-ceiling filing cabinets and floating pentagram sticky notes. He wears a tailored pinstripe suit unbuttoned to reveal a silk ascot, holding a silver ring light like a holy relic while his intense, slightly sweaty expression locks eyes with the viewer. Behind him, documentary crews pan across maze-like corridors where glowing holographic maps overlay the peeling yellow wallpaper. The lighting mixes cool fluorescents with warm, sultry amber highlights, casting long dramatic shadows that make the air feel thick and electric. Large bold movie title text reading "Bless This Lease: A True Crime Romance" spans the top in sleek metallic serif font, while a smaller tagline below reads "Some demons just need a permit." The composition feels painterly, saturated, and deeply unsettling yet irresistibly attractive.

films.title: The Peyote Palimpsest: A Suburban Odyssey
films.synopsis: Newly sober suburban dad Gary just wants to survive his HOA meetings without summoning his ancestors. But when a rogue batch of peyote-laced kombucha triggers a mythic desert uprising, he must ride across a hallucinatory wasteland to reclaim his lost family lineage before the dead start a civil war. Armed with a rusted lawnmower scythe and a crumpled sobriety chip, Gary battles weeping sphinxes and aggressively polite zombies in a quest to become a better father and a worse necromancer. Will he keep it together, or let the past literally chew him up?
films.pitch: Pitch: Acid Western (peyote-fueled) x Mythic Retelling. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Suburban Dad Necromancer newly sober and rage-y about it.
films.image_prompt: A wide cinematic movie poster features a gruff suburban dad in a stained polo shirt and cargo shorts standing defiantly against a sprawling psychedelic desert horizon where jagged saguaro cacti stretch like skeletal fingers into a bruised purple sky. He clutches a rusted lawnmower scythe in one hand and a glowing sobriety chip in the other, his expression caught between exhausted rage and terrifying determination. Behind him, translucent ancestral figures rise from cracked earth, their faces half-obscured by shimmering heat haze and peyote-fueled dream logic. Warm golden sunset light bleeds through swirling ochre dust, illuminating the scene in rich ambers, deep crimsons, and electric teals that evoke both biblical dread and surreal romance. Large bold serif movie title text reading "THE PEYOTE PALIMPSEST: A SUBURBAN ODYSSEY" spans the upper third, with a smaller italicized tagline beneath it declaring "Sobriety is just the beginning." The overall visual mood is painterly, hyper-detailed, and brilliantly absurd.

films.title: Tenure, Tentacles, and a Thousand Doors
films.synopsis: When a penitent demon returns to academia to finally complete his dissertation, he finds himself trapped in a Brutalist university wing where floor plans shift like living cartilage. As clerks demand signatures and tenure committees multiply through identical oak doors, his carefully controlled reincarnation begins to unravel grotesquely. His tailored blazer fuses to his skin, his eyebrows sprout wet cilia, and his thesis notes bleed from every drawer. With graduation day approaching, he must navigate an increasingly fleshy labyrinth of academic red tape before his own spine files its own grievance.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Farce (so many doors) in act one, Body Horror (Cronenberg-core) by act three, and a Reformed Demon who never finished their PhD sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A towering vintage movie poster featuring a hunched, horned academic demon clutching a styrofoam microwave dinner while weeping openly into the fogging plastic lid. His tailored tweed blazer has fused to his torso, rippling with wet, translucent cartilage and sprouting small velvet antlers from his collarbone. He stands in an endless institutional hallway lined with identical mahogany doors that bulge outward like breathing lungs, each doorframe weeping condensation. Cool fluorescent lighting casts long, dramatic shadows across checkered linoleum floors stained with faint organic fluids. The color palette mixes muted academic beige and slate gray with visceral pinks, bruised purples, and sickly neon greens. large bold movie title text reading "TENURE, TENTACLES, AND A THOUSAND DOORS" across the top in cracked serif lettering, with a smaller tagline beneath it that says "Graduation is just the beginning." The overall mood is dreadfully comedic, painterly, and visually saturated with body-horror texture.

films.title: The Knight in the Empty Courtyard
films.synopsis: Sir Reginald Vance has spent decades mastering jousting technique and dissecting Middle English alliterative verse, but a recent tourney victory accidentally drags him into an endless, fluorescent-lit corridor that smells faintly of polyester and regret. Realizing his quiet retirement is actually a sprawling liminal nightmare directed by a rogue cult documentary crew, Reginald must trade his lance for a lavalier microphone and weaponize narrative tropes to survive. Now he’s forced to interrogate faceless banner-wavers, decode ritualistic wayfinding clues in Chaucerian couplets, and edit his own descent into static before the final cut locks him in forever.
films.pitch: Logline: When a Renaissance Fair Champion with a doctorate in medieval poetry discovers their quiet life is actually a Liminal Space Horror, they must master the genre conventions of Cult Exposé to survive.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic poster centers on a bewildered Renaissance fair champion standing amid an endless, fluorescent-lit corridor where painted tapestry backdrops melt into sterile beige walls. He wears a tattered velvet doublet layered over a modern tweed blazer, clutching a vintage jousting lance in one hand and a lavalier microphone in the other while pointing at a floating parchment covered in dense medieval poetry. His expression shifts between scholarly focus and quiet dread as he studies the repeating architectural archways behind him. The lighting casts cool cyan shadows against warm amber highlights, creating an unsettling painterly atmosphere that blends documentary realism with surreal spatial distortion. Behind him, the silhouettes of a cult film crew glide silently through the haze. Large bold movie title text reading "THE KNIGHT IN THE EMPTY COURTYARD" spans the top in elegant serif typography, accompanied by a smaller tagline underneath that says "He studied the verse. The halls studied him back."

films.title: A Santa’s Long Road to Sin
films.synopsis: When a deflated mall Santa laces up for the Ironwood Village Marathon, he accidentally becomes the prime suspect in a suffocating family feud over the town’s ancestral textile mill. Trapped between his burning quads and an unexpectedly passionate courtship with the widowed mill owner, Arthur must solve the vicar’s bizarre bell tower disappearance before his marathon splits turn into a dynasty tragedy. As ancestral secrets unravel alongside his training log, he realizes this cozy parish hides scandals far more dangerous than poor nutrition. Every sprint toward the finish line drags another family ruin out of the snow.
films.pitch: Pitch: Cozy Village Whodunit x Family Saga. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Failed Mall Santa training for a marathon.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a weary but intensely focused man in his late forties sprinting through a snow-dusted English village square at twilight. He wears a faded, slightly stretched red mall Santa suit paired with neon orange running tights and expensive trail sneakers, his chest heaving with athletic dread as he locks eyes with a mysterious woman watching from a timber-framed doorway. Across the top, large bold serif text reads "A SANTA’S LONG ROAD TO SIN" in crisp white letters that cut through heavy atmospheric fog. Beneath it, smaller italicized text displays the tagline "Every step hides a secret." The background reveals cobblestone streets, glowing shop windows, and the shadowy silhouette of an ancestral manor clinging to a nearby hill. Moody blue and amber lighting bathes the scene in a painterly glow, emphasizing rich textures of wool, snow, and mist while evoking a mood of sensual mystery and quiet rural dread.

films.title: SQUELCH CITY SHADOWS: THE PREPPER PROTOCOL
films.synopsis: Martha Crumble, a doomsday prepper grandma fulfilling court-ordered community service in the squelching flesh-labs of Neo-Detroit, must solve the murder of a bio-engineered femme fatale who died screaming noir-style riddles. Slapping weeping organs with dentures and sobbing into a tinfoil-wrapped TV Dinner, Martha navigates walls that sweat blood and growl cynical monologues while hunting the culprit with nothing but an irradiated canteen and her signature tinfoil fedora. If she can't crack the genetic cipher before her meal beeps, the city's biological infrastructure will collapse into a gumbo of justice, gristle, and regret.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Hardboiled Detective in act one, Biopunk (squelchy laboratory) by act three, and a Doomsday Prepper Grandma doing court-ordered community service sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A painterly movie poster features Martha Crumble, a doomsday prepper grandma in a tinfoil fedora and wire glasses, slapping a weeping biological organ with one hand while clutching a canteen of green liquid and worrying over her microwave dinner inside her reinforced parka. She stands inside a squelching biopunk lab where pulsating flesh-wires drip sweat onto concrete floors under harsh noir lighting, casting long shadows around neon-green highlights that glow with cynical mystery. The walls of layered muscle and rusted metal weep condensation as if mourning a hardboiled tragedy, while Martha gazes forward with determined skepticism against a backdrop of dripping organic machinery. Large bold movie title text reading "SQUELCH CITY SHADOWS: THE PREPPER PROTOCOL" spans the top in distressed typewriter font, and a smaller tagline below reads "She served time, but she never ate the soup."

films.title: Please Return the World to Its Shelf
films.synopsis: When the sky gradually turns the color of dusty microfiche and gravity starts taking its weekends off, mild-mannered archivist Barnaby clings to his cubicle at the municipal library. Having memorized three hundred hours of algorithmic doomsday videos, he insists the end of civilization is simply a massive overdue fine waiting to be settled. As colleagues calmly pack their succulents and the ocean begins gently evaporating into pastel confetti, Barnaby must navigate union meetings, breakroom politics, and his boss’s inexplicable new tail to save the branch before it gets condemned. It is a hilarious comedy about maintaining routine while reality quietly unpacks itself.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Conspiracy-Theorist Librarian who learned everything from YouTube, alone, in the rain. The genre? Workplace Comedy. The vibe? Unmistakably Gentle Apocalypse. Cue title card.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a slightly disheveled librarian standing center frame inside an empty municipal library where bookshelves are slowly tilting upward toward a pastel sky visible through the rain-streaked glass windows. He wears a rumpled beige cardigan over a graphic t-shirt displaying a cartoonish alien, and his wide conspiratorial eyes lock directly onto the viewer while he clutches a plastic barcode scanner like a sacred relic. The warm amber glow of vintage desk lamps fights against the soft lavender and muted gold lighting of an unfolding gentle apocalypse, casting long dreamlike shadows across scattered paperwork and wilting ferns. Large bold movie title text reading "PLEASE RETURN THE WORLD TO ITS SHELF" spans the top third of the composition, with a smaller understated tagline underneath that reads "Some fines are overdue." The overall visual mood is deeply painterly, whimsically surreal, and quietly absurd.

films.title: A Gentleman's Guide to Gridiron Ghosts
films.synopsis: Meet Gary, a mild-mannered suburban dad who secretly commands legions of freshly unearthed ghosts to mow his lawn and help with PTA meetings. When an anonymous love letter arrives from a reanimated Regency duke, Gary must navigate the cutthroat world of neighborhood bocce tournaments while chaperoning a whirlwind romance that literally rises from the earth. With his secret grave-robbing habits threatening to derail both his presidency and his newfound passion, Gary discovers that true love might just be a matter of coming back to life.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Sports Doc show had a baby with a Regency Bodice-Ripper miniseries, and that baby grew up to be a Suburban Dad Necromancer with a heart of gold.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a weary but beaming suburban father in his forties wearing a crisp white polo shirt tucked neatly into khaki slacks, standing proudly beside a rusted minivan that has sprouted delicate lace curtains and velvet drapes. He holds a bocce ball in one hand and an antique quill in the other, while translucent Victorian ghosts in tuxedos and crinoline gowns float playfully around him against a backdrop of perfectly manicured suburban lawns merging with foggy Regency-era manor gates. The lighting is warm and golden hour soft, casting long dramatic shadows across dew-kissed grass, rendered in rich emerald greens, powder blues, and cream tones with a painterly, romantic documentary aesthetic. Large bold serif title text reading "A GENTLEMAN'S GUIDE TO GRIDIRON GHOSTS" spans the top third of the composition, with a smaller elegant tagline beneath that says "Some loves rise from the earth."

films.title: The Queue Beyond the Vein
films.synopsis: When immortal call-center operative Arthur discovers his ergonomic chair has slowly fused with his lumbar spine, he realizes the fluorescent-lit cubicle farm is actually a pulsating gateway to another dimension. Trapped between corporate metrics and eldritch geometry, Arthur must navigate treacherous portal-fantasy conventions while communicating exclusively through classic movie quotes. With colleagues’ faces peeling into secondary mouths and the help desk expanding into a carnivorous abyss, survival depends on mastering both the monster manual and the script database before his next performance review begins.
films.pitch: Logline: When a Call Center Vampire who only speaks in movie quotes discovers their quiet life is actually a Body Horror (Cronenberg-core), they must master the genre conventions of Portal Fantasy to survive.
films.image_prompt: A towering movie poster depicts a gaunt, pale man in a slightly stained business casual shirt and loosened tie standing at the edge of a grotesque yet beautiful fleshy portal that ripples like wet muscle tissue behind him. His expression is one of weary determination mixed with mild existential dread, his eyes reflecting glowing corporate spreadsheet data alongside swirling otherworldly constellations. The setting blends a sterile office cubicle maze with writhing tentacles and bioluminescent vein networks, all illuminated by harsh fluorescent overhead lighting that casts long dramatic shadows across the scene. Large bold movie title text reading "THE QUEUE BEYOND THE VEIN" spans the top in weathered metallic lettering, while a smaller tagline beneath reads "Every call connects to another dimension." The color palette features sickly greens, bruised purples, and sterile whites, creating a painterly, Cronenberg-meets-D&D atmosphere that feels both deeply uncomfortable and wildly adventurous.

films.title: PIRATE OF THE PAVEMENT: TEARS AND BISCUITS
films.synopsis: Shot entirely on a bruised JVC camcorder, this neo-western follows Captain Barnaby, a lovable himbo pirate who traded his ship for running shoes to outrun a mysterious debt collector made of corgis. As he drags his bleeding feet across the unforgiving asphalt frontier, the footage degrades into pure chaos, capturing his desperate sobbing into soggy microwave biscuits while hallucinating parrots that judge his stride. It's a 26.2-mile odyssey of heart, humor, and questionable life choices where the horizon is just a finish line he might never reach.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Found Footage in act one, Modern Neo-Western by act three, and a Himbo Pirate training for a marathon sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring Captain Barnaby, a muscular himbo pirate with goofy teeth and glistening sweat, sprinting down a cracked desert highway that stretches toward a blood-orange sun. He wears spandex running tights over a torn striped shirt, an eye patch covering one determined eye, and heavy-duty running shoes made of whale leather, clutching a steaming microwave dinner to his cheek as he weeps with exhaustion. His expression is a mix of adorable panic and unyielding willpower, while in the dusty background, shadowy corgis loom like western outlaws ready to chase him down. The color palette is gritty teal and orange with high-contrast lighting that emphasizes the texture of his skin and the steam rising from his hot meal. Large bold movie title text reading "PIRATE OF THE PAVEMENT: TEARS AND BISCUITS" anchors the top in a distressed western font, with a smaller tagline below reading "A Found Footage Marathon Epic."

films.title: The Howling Lute and Honeyed Dread
films.synopsis: When a deeply neurotic werewolf discovers that polyphonic lute covers of modern pop songs calm his transformation anxiety, he retreats to an isolated mountain estate to build the perfect apiary. But his peaceful bee-keeping ritual quickly spirals into a bizarre new faith when his honeycombs begin pulsing with an ancient, rhythmic drone. Investigative filmmakers track down his cult-like followers, capturing their sweat-slicked, lute-strumming processions under a blood-red moon. Now he must conduct his first hive wedding before the next full moon turns his bride into another anxious, humming acolyte.
films.pitch: Pitch: Bardcore Medieval x Cult Exposé. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Anxious Werewolf raising bees as therapy.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster capturing a deeply anxious werewolf with fur dusted in golden pollen, wearing a frayed velvet bard’s tunic while nervously strumming a lute beside an overflowing apiary table. He stands at the center of a misty medieval stone courtyard surrounded by glowing honeycombs that pulse with an otherworldly amber light. Behind him, a devoted cult of followers in matching linen tunics chant and play stringed instruments under a bruised purple moon. The scene is bathed in moody, intimate lighting that blends warm candlelit tones with cool silver lunar hues to create a strangely romantic yet unsettling atmosphere. Large bold serif title text reading "The Howling Lute and Honeyed Dread" spans the top in weathered gold lettering, while a smaller handwritten tagline underneath says "Some rituals are hard to resist."

films.title: Oyez! The Fluorescent Void Demands Forms
films.synopsis: After a fatal mispronunciation costs him his reputation, a penitent medieval town crier is yeeted into the infinite yellow hallways of the Backrooms. He quickly learns this liminal office complex is actually an eldritch filing system run by a slumbering god of bureaucratic madness. To earn his mortal return, he must navigate non-Euclidean cubicles and file Form 666-B before cosmic auditors devour his soul. Armed only with a brass megaphone and a quill dipped in void ink, he prepares to proclaim the end of reality itself.
films.pitch: A Medieval Town Crier seeking redemption stumbles into a Backrooms Bureaucracy conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Cosmic / Lovecraftian.
films.image_prompt: A dramatic cinematic movie poster featuring a weary medieval town crier standing at the center of an endless, perspective-twisting hallway of peeling yellow wallpaper and flickering fluorescent panels that stretch into a non-Euclidean void. He wears a tattered leather jerkin with chainmail accents, brass buttons catching the sickly greenish light, and holds a tarnished brass megaphone to his lips while clutching a stack of parchment forms in his other hand. His expression is one of exhausted determination mixed with dawning cosmic terror as shadowy tentacle-like filing cabinets rise from the linoleum floor behind him. The lighting casts long, impossible shadows that curve upward into spiral staircases leading to nowhere. Large bold white movie title text reading "OYEZ! THE FLUORESCENT VOID DEMANDS FORMS" spans across the top in a clean serif font, with a smaller tagline beneath it that reads "Every Proclamation Ends in Madness." The overall color palette balances mustard yellow and sterile office beige with deep cosmic purples bleeding through the distant architecture.

films.title: FOUR-LEGGED FARE: EATING UP THE VOID
films.synopsis: Centaur rideshare driver Barnaby survives on antihistamines while battling a severe allergy to his own profession, which manifests as spontaneous hives whenever his GPS recalculates. His latest fare transports him through a dimensional rift directly into the boardroom of an intergalactic wealth conglomerate that consumes human anxiety to inflate their stock prices. To escape the void, Barnaby must weaponize corporate absurdity, forcing eldritch executives to pay for their own cosmic erasure through aggressive surge pricing and premium tipping protocols. A fur-flung road trip where satire becomes the only armor against madness.
films.pitch: A Rideshare-Driving Centaur who is allergic to their own job stumbles into a Cosmic / Lovecraftian conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Satire (eat the rich).
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a rumpled centaur chauffeur wearing a neon reflective vest and a crumpled paper hat, his lower horse body covered in bizarre red hives from working too hard. He sits behind the wheel of a sleek black luxury sedan that is somehow floating through a swirling nebula of gold coins and weeping eldritch eyes. The centaur holds a rideshare tablet like a holy relic, his expression caught between exhaustion and terrified realization as cosmic tentacles made of credit cards wrap around the dashboard. Warm amber interior lighting clashes with deep violet and bruised purple space outside, creating a painterly mood of surreal corporate horror. Large bold serif movie title text reading "FOUR-LEGGED FARE: EATING UP THE VOID" spans the top third of the poster, with a smaller tagline underneath that reads "Surge pricing has never been this existential."

films.title: HARK! THE ARCHIVE MELTS MID-FLUSH
films.synopsis: When the aristocracy begins paying property taxes in degraded VHS tape, a perpetually flustered medieval town crier named Beatrice discovers her daily proclamations are literally summoning tax-exempt phantoms. Battling sudden hot flashes and deteriorating reality, she must ring her iron bell through the static-choked market square before the billionaires’ forgotten fortune drains into the ether. With each flush of heat comes a sharper truth about the wealthy, turning a mundane census into a surreal crusade against gilded ghosts. It is a razor-sharp satire wrapped in magnetic tape, where menopause meets hauntology and every chime costs a memory.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Satire (eat the rich) and Hauntology (lost media core) coexist uneasily, our hero — a Medieval Town Crier going through perimenopause — must save... something. Probably a town.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring an exhausted medieval town crier standing center stage in a cobblestone marketplace that slowly dissolves into floating VHS magnetic tape and shredded tax receipts. The protagonist wears a heavy woolen doublet stained with sweat and ink, clutching a tarnished brass bell while her face contorts between fierce proclamation and sudden, bewildered heat flashes. Behind her, towering gilded aristocrats float like translucent phantoms, their wealth literally melting into analog static that drifts upward like steam. The lighting is dramatic and high-contrast, casting deep amber shadows against a palette of burnt crimson, faded teal, and oxidized gold. Large bold movie title text reading "HARK! THE ARCHIVE MELTS MID-FLUSH" spans the top in distressed serif typography, with a smaller tagline beneath that reads "Every chime costs a memory." The overall mood is painterly, surreal, and darkly comedic, blending medieval texture with analog decay.

films.title: The YouTube Werewolf of Iron Harbor
films.synopsis: In the soot-choked metropolis of Iron Harbor, a neurotic werewolf has spent months mastering transformation triggers via video tutorials. But when a corrupt diesel magnate’s mechanized hounds flatten his prized poodle, his anxiety spirals into a meticulously researched quest for vengeance. Armed with a cracked tablet and an unhealthy reliance on unverified survival hacks, he tracks the industrial tycoon through steam-filled alleyways and brass-clad skyscrapers. Now, he must decide whether to transform mid-tutorial or risk everything in a howl-off that could tear the city’s pressure valves apart.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Dieselpunk and Revenge Thriller (he killed my dog) coexist uneasily, our hero — a Anxious Werewolf who learned everything from YouTube — must save... something. Probably a town.
films.image_prompt: A highly detailed cinematic movie poster features a lanky, anxious werewolf wearing a patched leather aviator cap and brass goggles pushed up on his forehead while frantically swiping through a cracked tablet with clawed paws. He stands atop a rusted diesel engine in a sprawling smog-choked cityscape filled with towering brass gears and massive iron pipelines glowing under amber streetlamps. Dramatic lighting casts long shadows across his fur, highlighting a mixture of sweaty panic and fierce determination on his face. The color palette is dominated by sepia, oxidized copper green, and deep charcoal grays, creating a gritty painterly mood. Across the top in large bold vintage typography reads "The YouTube Werewolf of Iron Harbor", with a smaller tagline beneath it reading "He watched three videos and now he's howling." The composition blends mechanical precision with primal fur texture.

films.title: The Blender Deposition: A Cold Case Haunting
films.synopsis: Suspicious audio journalist investigates the "Kitchen Sink Massacre" only to discover a legalese-speaking ghost has possessed the local smart-blender, screaming binding verdicts in smoothie form. The spirit delivers prophetic dreams where he serves subpoenas to corpses, revealing that every domestic dispute is actually a prelude to murder. To crack the cold case before the warranty expires, she must appease the poltergeist with unbleached parchment and argue her way through a spectral trial where justice is served at 40,000 RPM.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Cold Case Podcast Mystery show had a baby with a Haunted Appliance miniseries, and that baby grew up to be a Legalese-Speaking Ghost with prophetic dreams.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a sleek smart-blender on a dark kitchen countertop, its base pulsing with an ominous neon red light that illuminates swirling spectral mist rising from the glass pitcher. Inside the swirling mist, a translucent ghost wearing a tattered black lawyer's robe and a powdered wig emerges, holding a tiny silver gavel while screaming legal jargon in visible sound waves that look like scrolling contract text. The ghost's eyes glow with prophetic amber fire, and behind him loom faint images of floating vegetables like evidence exhibits. The background is a moody, desaturated kitchen with stainless steel appliances watching like silent jurors. Large bold title text reading "THE BLENDER DEPOSITION" spans the top in heavy serif font, with subtitle text reading "A COLD CASE HAUNTING" just below it in stark white. Tagline text at the bottom reads "JUSTICE IS SERVED AT 40,000 RPM." The visual mood is a blend of noir suspense and domestic absurdity, rendered in hyper-realistic digital painting style with sharp focus on the ghost's litigious expression.

films.title: DUST, BONES AND BYLAWS
films.synopsis: In the dusty frontier town of Oak Creek, where ghouls patrol the cul-de-sacs and saloon wars are decided by gunfights over property lines, Arch-Lich Zorgul has ruled as HOA President for three millennia without a single violation filed. But unbeknownst to his undead neighbors, Zorgul was replaced eons ago by a shapeshifting doppelgänger named Kevin who is terrifyingly good at mowing lawns and enforcing the shade of beige permissible on front porches. Now, when an ancient mythic prophecy threatens to dissolve the suburb into primordial chaos, Kevin must wield his clipboard and a revolver loaded with holy water to prove he's still the Lich everyone fears, even if he does have a minor dispute over Gary's gazebo height.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Mythic Retelling show had a baby with a Weird West (cowboys + monsters) miniseries, and that baby grew up to be a HOA-President Lich who has been replaced by a doppelgänger and nobody noticed.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster for a weird western mythic retelling featuring the protagonist standing confidently in the center of a dusty frontier main street that seamlessly transitions into a sterile suburban cul-de-sac filled with tumbleweeds and lawn ornaments. The character is an uncanny blend of bureaucracy and undeath, wearing a pristine white pinstripe suit with a skull-shaped tie clip, holding a clipboard in one skeletal hand and a smoking six-shooter in the other, while their face shifts from a handsome human doppelgänger to a grinning necrotic visage. The background shows a saloon with an HOA sign swinging above the doors, bathed in dramatic golden-hour lighting that casts long shadows across the cracked earth. Large bold serif movie title text reading "DUST, BONES AND BYLAWS" dominates the top of the poster, with a tagline below reading "The Bylaws Are Eternal." The color palette is saturated ochre, blood red, and bone white, evoking a painterly, epic yet absurd tone.

films.title: THE LAST HIVE OF CHRISTMAS GULCH
films.synopsis: After a catastrophic honeycomb audit ruins his marriage and reputation, disgraced Bee Inspector Silas Vance retreats to frostbitten Christmas Gulch for the holidays. He quickly discovers the quiet mining town is overrun by prehistoric, antlered grizzly-lemurs that demand seasonal tribute in exchange for stopping their festive stampedes. When his soon-to-be-ex files an emergency injunction via stagecoach, Silas must deploy his trusty smoker, a borrowed tuxedo vest, and his last intact hive to win back both the town’s annual Yule bell festival and his ex-wife’s reluctant heart. It’s a messy divorce, a monstrous matriarch, and one very sticky road to reconciliation.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Weird West (cowboys + monsters) and Small-Town Christmas Romance coexist uneasily, our hero — a Disgraced Bee Inspector going through a messy divorce — must save... something. Probably a town.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic poster painting showing a disheveled man in his forties wearing a rumpled velvet smoking jacket over a duster coat, with a cowboy hat tilted low and a half-eaten candy cane hooked on his belt. He grips a brass bee smoker like a sidearm, his expression caught between exhausted resignation and desperate hope as he faces the viewer. Behind him, a snow-dusted frontier town glows under bruised purple twilight, while monstrous antlered grizzly-lemurs lurk in the frosty timberline shadows. Large bold serif text reading "THE LAST HIVE OF CHRISTMAS GULCH" spans the top third of the image, accompanied by a smaller elegant tagline that says "Love stings when you least expect it." The scene uses rich crimson and gold accents against deep indigo snow, creating a painterly mood that perfectly blends western grit with cozy holiday romance.

films.title: THE GRIM GRANTOR TRUST OF GHOST RIDGE
films.synopsis: In the perpetually weeping badlands of Gray Gulch, where the sun is a bruised eye and the rain tastes like regret, Barnaby Witherby drifts as a translucent entity fluent only in archaic contract law. He's running the afterlife's first Ponzi scheme by promising dead souls guaranteed reincarnation in a tax-advantaged paradise, but he keeps paying old retirees with fresh arrivals' ectoplasm while hallucinating peyote-induced market crashes. Now that the cosmic SEC has sent a demon auditor to audit his liquidity ratios, Barnaby must defend his empire of lies using a loophole hidden in the fine print of existence itself before reality dissolves into absolute void.
films.pitch: It's Grimdark (everyone's sad and damp) meets Acid Western (peyote-fueled)... but the protagonist is a Legalese-Speaking Ghost secretly running a Ponzi scheme.
films.image_prompt: A hauntingly surreal movie poster depicting a translucent, mournful ghost wearing a tattered Victorian lawyer's wig and a poncho woven from crumpled legal documents, standing knee-deep in iridescent mud that reflects peyote-induced fractals. The background is a desolate, dripping Western canyon landscape painted in deep indigos and bruised purples, where cacti pulse with bioluminescent veins and the sky weeps heavy silver rain. The ghost holds a quill that drips glowing ink, his expression one of weary existential dread mixed with manic financial optimism. Lighting is moody and atmospheric, with shafts of sickly green moonlight cutting through the mist, highlighting the texture of damp fog and shimmering ectoplasm. Large bold movie title text reading "THE GRIM GRANTOR TRUST OF GHOST RIDGE" arches across the top in distressed serif font, with a tagline underneath reading "Your soul is temporarily liquid."

films.title: The Zoning Committee of Drowning Suburbs
films.synopsis: When a municipal zoning error causes the town’s bedrock to literally give up and slide into the riverbed, reclusive underworld clerk Arthur Pemble must investigate the slow-motion apocalypse. Haunted by PTSD triggered by expired stamps and damp manila folders, he treats cadastral decay like a murder board, mapping shifting property lines with obsessive red string and terrified intensity. As water tables rise and permits multiply like rats, Arthur realizes the real killer isn’t geology—it’s bad file storage. He has exactly three fiscal quarters to stamp “Approved” on salvation before the entire district dissolves into bureaucratic slush.
films.genre_2_sub: True Crime (let me look at this map)
films.pitch: Set in a world where Mundane Apocalypse and True Crime (let me look at this map) coexist uneasily, our hero — a Underworld Bureaucrat with PTSD — must save... something. Probably a town.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster bathed in moody teal and rust-orange lighting depicts a weary middle-aged man standing knee-deep in an underground municipal archive that is slowly tilting into a massive sinkhole. The protagonist wears a damp charcoal trench coat over a wrinkled beige suit, clutching a leather briefcase and a giant rubber stamp like weapons against the encroaching dampness. His expression shows exhausted paranoia, with eyes darting toward floating stacks of paperwork and a tangled mess of red investigator’s string stretched across collapsing concrete pillars. Towering metal filing cabinets act as shoring timbers while faint municipal floodwater laps at his polished shoes. Large bold white title text reading "THE ZONING COMMITTEE OF DROWNING SUBURBS" spans the upper third, with a smaller tagline underneath saying "Every district has a body count." The overall mood is painterly and suspenseful, blending documentary grit with surreal environmental dread.

films.title: Per My Last Prophecy
films.synopsis: Assigned to investigate a missing levitation wand, reluctant middle manager Arthur must decode parchment trails and audit enchanted pastry receipts before the kingdom’s infrastructure collapses under conflicting magical bylaws. He discovers the real culprit is actually the castle head baker, whose sourdough shortcuts are bypassing zoning laws and threatening his quarterly soul-collection bonus. With his family group chat still aggressively on read and three minor demons filing grievances over his expense reports, Arthur has no choice but to solve the case from his cubicle using only a stapler, a magnifying glass, and pure bureaucratic panic.
films.pitch: Imagine if a Bureaucratic Fantasy (the Wizard's HR dept) show had a baby with a Amateur Sleuth (a baker did it) miniseries, and that baby grew up to be a Reluctant Middle Manager currently ghosting their family group chat.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster features a weary middle manager in a rumpled charcoal suit adorned with glowing bureaucratic rune pins, standing amidst towering shelves of floating parchment and ancient magical artifacts repurposed as office supplies. He holds a smartphone displaying an aggressive unread notification badge while gripping a heavy iron stapler like a detective’s magnifying glass. The scene is bathed in dramatic volumetric lighting that blends warm stained-glass golden hour with sterile fluorescent tubes, casting long shadows across checkered marble floors etched with zoning diagrams. Deep indigo and muted emerald tones dominate the composition, evoking a cozy yet absurdly tense corporate fantasy atmosphere. Large bold movie title text reading "PER MY LAST PROPHECY" spans the top in crisp serif typography, while a smaller tagline underneath declares "AUDIT THE ARCANE." The image is rendered in a painterly digital illustration style with sharp focus on the protagonist's exhausted but determined expression.

films.title: Sanctum Scramble: The Seagull Syndrome
films.synopsis: When a single audacious seagull drops its morning business onto the Sistine Chapel ceiling, a reluctant Pope abandons his throne for a citywide leap-and-leap frenzy. Bouncing across slick rooftops and sliding under dripping awnings, the Pontiff must navigate a visceral obstacle course where every jump triggers a new wave of improbable condiments, sewage sprays, and squamous splashes. His pursuit against this feathered nemesis becomes a gruesome test of acrobatic grace and gastrointestinal endurance. With the coronation bells approaching, he must master the art of the acrobatic splash zone before his vestments—and his soul—are completely ruined.
films.pitch: Picture a Parkour Chase Movie setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Gross-Out. Our reluctant hero: a Reluctant Pope with a vendetta against a specific seagull.
films.image_prompt: A dramatic low-angle poster features a weary Pope mid-leap across rain-slicked cobblestones, his heavy black cassock flapping as he kicks up neon-yellow sludge and thick gravy rivers. His face wears an exhausted grimace, eyes locked on a grotesquely oversized seagull perched atop a dripping marble fountain like an archangel of chaos. The stylized plaza background gushes with exaggerated puddles under dramatic golden-hour lighting that paints the scene in vivid crimson, electric blue, and sickly chartreuse tones. Large bold movie title text reading "Sanctum Scramble: The Seagull Syndrome" arches across the top in cracked marble lettering, with a smaller tagline underneath proclaiming grace is fleeting but the mess is eternal. The composition blends sacred solemnity with cartoonish gastrointestinal mayhem, rendered in thick impasto brushstrokes that capture both athletic tension and visceral comedy.

films.title: Tinfoil Therapy: The Caped Counselor's Breakdown
films.synopsis: When a hyper-anxious superhero therapist agrees to document his own crisis sessions for a quirky streaming docuseries, his handheld camera slowly turns into a vessel for escalating dread and delirious snacks. As real-world villains target his apartment building, the footage seamlessly devolves from tense found-footage horror into a deeply personal stoner comedy about capes, cortisol, and aggressively microwaved burritos. Now he must confront his biggest nemesis yet: the terrifying prospect of paying rent while sobbing into instant noodles over a camcorder lens.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Found Footage in act one, Stoner Comedy by act three, and a Superhero Therapist with crippling anxiety sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A grainy camcorder screen dominates the poster, reflecting the terrified face of a superhero therapist wearing a frayed spandex cape over oversized cardigan sweatpants and thick prescription glasses. His expression mixes wide-eyed panic with exhausted resignation as he presses a steaming microwave burrito to his cheek while a crinkled bag of chips spills across a coffee table cluttered with therapy books and half-drunk soda cans. The background transitions from shadowy horror silhouettes at the top into a warm, hazy living room at the bottom, bathed in flickering television glow and soft neon blue accents that emphasize the absurd mood. Large bold movie title text reading "TINFOIL THERAPY: THE CAPED COUNSELOR'S BREAKDOWN" spans across the middle of the composition, with a smaller tagline beneath it that says "I'm not okay, but I brought snacks." The entire scene is rendered in a painterly cinematic style with high contrast lighting and vivid saturated colors.

films.title: A Lament for Leaky Roofs
films.synopsis: When a thoroughly average knight inherits the damp, specter-infested Blackwood Manor, he quickly learns that haunting is less about jump scares and more about compound interest on structural decay. As phantom drafts seep through the floorboards and his ancestral four-poster develops an unsettlingly intimate sighing habit, Brumley begins channeling his existential dread into acoustically perfect folk ballads about load-bearing walls and repressed trauma. Now caught between a spectral tenant union strike and a poltergeist that rearranges his cutlery by touch, he must decide whether to sell the property, marry the house, or finally confront why he only breaks into three-part harmony when terrified.
films.pitch: Pitch: Sad Indie Folk Musical x Prestige Misery Drama. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Mediocre Knight who just inherited a haunted mansion.
films.image_prompt: A moody, painterly movie poster features a lanky, perpetually tired knight in tarnished chainmail and a damp wool cloak standing alone in the center of a cavernous, gothic manor foyer. The stone floor is scattered with floating lease documents, dried lavender sprigs that double as makeshift microphones, and a single velvet chaise lounge pulled slightly askew. He gazes upward with a deeply vulnerable, breathless expression, one hand pressed to his sternum while the other cradles a weathered acoustic guitar. Tall arched windows cast long, melancholic blue shadows across cracked marble tiles, creating an atmosphere of heavy, romantic dread. Large bold serif movie title text reading "A Lament for Leaky Roofs" spans the upper third of the composition in muted silver lettering, while a smaller elegant tagline beneath it reads "Some foundations are haunted, others just need therapy."

films.title: Molar Mayhem: The Talking Horse Campaigns for Squelch
films.synopsis: Barnaby, a talking horse with pulsating wetware ports drilled into his muzzle and a tongue acting as an IV drip, campaigns for mayor of Squelch Creek, where ballots dissolve instantly in acidic saliva. His rival is a gelatinous mass of fused incisors piloted by a rogue orthodontist drone, promising aggressive whitening at the cost of your soul's enamel. As Barnaby rallies voters to fix the town's failing root canal infrastructure before it collapses into organic sludge, he must survive a debate on a grinding molar stage while dodging dental drills fired by rival pollsters.
films.pitch: Picture a Dental Horror setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Biopunk (squelchy laboratory). Our reluctant hero: a Talking Horse running for local office.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring Barnaby, a rugged talking horse with pulsating biopunk wetware ports drilled into his muzzle and a fleshy IV tube snaking to a bone campaign medallion. He wears a tattered velvet political sash that writhes like living muscle across his chest while standing on a stage of fused tooth roots inside a damp, squelchy laboratory where giant dental drills spin like biological turbines. Barnaby gazes with sweaty determination toward a gelatinous pile of competing incisors, his mouth slightly open to reveal glowing fangs dripping enamel slurry amidst bioluminescent green and bruised purple mist. The title "MOLAR MAYHEM: THE TALKING HORSE CAMPAIGNS FOR SQUELCH" appears in bold, serrated typography carved from hardened tooth enamel across the top. A tagline below reads "HIS WORDS ARE AS SHARP AS HIS ROOT CANALS." The lighting is high-contrast and gooey, emphasizing slime textures and glistening organic machinery.

films.title: THE BEIGE HORIZON
films.synopsis: When a stand-up plague doctor’s beak microphone fails during his court-ordered community service, he is exiled to Sector Nine—a liminal desert where fluorescent filing cabinets sprout from the dunes and gunfights hinge on proper paperwork. Forced to trade his comedic riffs for tax codes, he must navigate endless beige cubicle canyons and win the annual Audit before total existential liquidation erases the frontier forever. With his punchlines dying faster than his pension, he has one last set to perform while dodging both bullet-riddled manila folders and the sheriff of forms.
films.pitch: Set in a world where Spaghetti Western and Backrooms Bureaucracy coexist uneasily, our hero — a Stand-Up Plague Doctor doing court-ordered community service — must save... something. Probably a town.
films.image_prompt: A wide cinematic movie poster featuring a lone stand-up plague doctor standing center stage in a sun-baked desert that seamlessly merges with endless beige office cubicles. He wears a cracked, leather turtleneck plague mask with a vintage microphone tucked into the beak, paired with a dusty duster coat and polished black boots, his expression a mix of weary resignation and manic comedic timing. Behind him, towering filing cabinets stretch into a hazy fluorescent sky, while shadows of cowboy silhouettes overlap with corporate water coolers and stamping desks. The lighting is high-contrast and painterly, casting long dramatic westward glows in burnt orange and sterile white against deep shadow tones. Large bold movie title text reading "THE BEIGE HORIZON" spans the top third, with a smaller tagline underneath that says "COURT-ORDERED COMEDY AT DEAD END DRIVE".

films.title: Pop Quiz: Covert Operations
films.synopsis: When earnest substitute teacher Arthur Pendelton wins a mysterious lottery, he’s thrust into The Long-Term Sub Protocol, a reality competition where educators complete espionage challenges between bell schedules. Armed with endless patience, Arthur navigates booby-trapped staff rooms and survives laser-stapler duels while quietly sobbing into his weekly microwave burrito. But when the rival gym coach reveals herself as a double agent, Arthur must choose between the championship trophy or saving the district’s entire fifth-grade cohort from permanent detention. Expect confession-cam monologues, sudden action set pieces, and a man who genuinely believes every child deserves a hug.
films.pitch: Three-act structure: Reality Competition in act one, Spy Thriller by act three, and a Substitute Teacher with a heart of gold sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
films.image_prompt: A dramatic cinematic movie poster featuring an earnest substitute teacher standing in a dimly lit school hallway that doubles as a reality television set. He wears a slightly wrinkled cardigan over a crisp button-down shirt, clutching a steaming microwave dinner bowl close to his chest while gripping a rolled-up lesson plan like a tactical map. His expression blends tearful determination with gentle kindness, illuminated by warm amber spy-thriller rim lighting clashing with cold blue confessional camera rings. Behind him, floating holographic grade books hover near ceiling-mounted surveillance cameras disguised as chalkboards, while blurred figures in tactical turtlenecks carry briefcases down the corridor. Large bold white movie title text reading "POP QUIZ: COVERT OPERATIONS" spans across the top third of the composition, with a smaller tagline beneath it that reads "Pass the spyware." The overall visual mood is painterly and highly detailed, capturing a hilarious yet intense crossover of documentary realism and globe-trotting espionage.

films.title: THE THAUMIC OMEGA: FORMS OF STRUCTURAL RUIN AND RUINED LIPS
films.synopsis: Gary "The Perpendicular Man" Sterling, an off-brand hero whose power to slightly dampen concrete caused a catastrophic vibration event during the Siege of Suburbia, now works in the Thaumic Spire's Department of Reality Variance. Here, magic is just unoptimized engineering and every wizard is haunted by the tyranny of Form 7B-Alpha while the ambient mana pressure slowly turns their nerves into wet, humming strings ready to snap or sing. Suffering from severe PTSD triggered by the rhythmic thumping of the soul-sorter press, Gary must manually calibrate the Spire's chaotic heart-engine using only a stapler and sheer desperation as the walls begin to breathe with erotic geometric precision. If he misses the quota, the reality-dread will melt his face off in slow motion, but the HR rep insists the resulting splatter pattern qualifies for a very hot performance bonus.
films.pitch: Pitch: Hard Sci-Fi (engineers explaining things) x Bureaucratic Fantasy (the Wizard's HR dept). Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Off-Brand Superhero with PTSD.
films.image_prompt: A cinematic movie poster featuring a weary off-brand superhero named The Perpendicular Man standing amidst a vast chamber where massive grinding gears merge with swirling runic circuits, the lighting is humid and claustrophobic with shafts of violet light illuminating steam rising from the machinery like breath. Gary wears an ill-fitting silver pressure suit stained with glowing blue mana-ink and bureaucratic staples, his face contorted in a mix of terror and intense longing as he stares at a floating holographic form that resembles a pulsating vein while clutching a heavy iron stapler to his chest. The background reveals endless stacks of parchment filing cabinets dissolving into chrome server racks, creating a sense of infinite administrative dread that feels strangely intimate and pressurized. Large bold metallic sans-serif text reading "THE THAUMIC OMEGA" appears across the top, with a subtitle below reading "FORMS OF STRUCTURAL RUIN AND RUINED LIPS" rendered in wet, glossy crimson type that looks freshly printed on skin. The color palette is deep indigo, electric cyan, and bruised purple, emphasizing the tension between cold engineering and visceral magical biology.

films.title: THE TREMBLING TRACKER OF TIN CANYON
films.synopsis: When his meticulously cataloged field notes are stolen by a smug rival with a louder steam-whistle, an increasingly jittery cryptozoologist must ride his boiler-powered bronco into the gaslight-soaked badlands. Teaming up with a coal-fueled mechanical sheriff, he navigates neon-drenched saloons where cowboys duel shadow-beasts with electrified lassos. But as the sky cracks open to reveal a constellation of terrified cryptids, our hero must finally confront his own nervous breakdown before the rivalry turns into a rusted tin apocalypse.
films.pitch: Picture a Dieselpunk setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Weird West (cowboys + monsters). Our reluctant hero: a Anxious Cryptozoologist with a rival for some reason.
films.image_prompt: A wide cinematic movie poster bathed in the amber and oxidized copper glow of a dieselpunk weird west frontier. At the center stands an anxious cryptozoologist wearing a puffy leather aviator helmet wired to a hissing brass steam-pack, his face frozen in a comically wide-eyed expression as he clutches a trembling field journal against his chest. He is perched atop a heavily modified gear-driven mechanical bronco that chokes thick black smoke against a backdrop of jagged tin mesas and flickering neon gas lamps. Bold distressed typography reading "THE TREMBLING TRACKER OF TIN CANYON" spans the upper sky in heavy metallic lettering, while a smaller tagline below declares a silent scream of impending discovery. The composition is rendered in rich oil-canvas textures with deep rusts, electric blues, and sepia tones, evoking a mood of beautifully chaotic existential dread meets frontier comedy.

films.title: Neon Dust and the Mediocre Oracle
films.synopsis: When a famously mediocre oracle named Silas is pulled from neon-lit witness protection, he’s forced to ride west across a rain-drenched frontier where corporate ranchers control the water supply and every townie sports a cybernetic mustache. Silas doesn’t see the future clearly, mostly predicting minor inconveniences like bad noodles and delayed trains, but when his vaguer-than-usual visions suddenly point to an apocalyptic server storm, he must outwit a band of highly competent bounty hunters who take their prophecies too literally. Blending moral ambiguity with flickering neon saloons, this gritty yet hilarious showdown asks whether destiny matters when your main gift is guessing the weather wrong.
films.pitch: Cold open: a Mediocre Oracle in witness protection, alone, in the rain. The genre? Revisionist Western. The vibe? Unmistakably Cyberpunk (neon and noodles). Cue title card.
films.image_prompt: A wide cinematic movie poster depicting a lone, weary figure standing knee-deep in neon-soaked puddles outside a rusted steel saloon on the edge of a sprawling cyber-western frontier. The central character wears a frayed leather duster coat over faded synthetic denim, with a tattered fedora pulled low over tired eyes that hold the faint glow of a cracked visor.
